 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yff.7","title":"E2E: Twitter/X Connector Compliance Run","description":"# E2E: Twitter/X Connector Compliance Run

## Goal
Add Twitter/X scenarios to the shared compliance harness.

## Scenarios
- Default deny.
- Allow with valid token.
- Network guard allow/deny.
- Streaming connection behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo test --package fcp-e2e --features twitter` passes deterministically.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ykjm","title":"[IDEA-02.3] RateLimitDeclarations: host discovery + CLI surfacing","description":"## Goal
Surface rate limit declarations/status via host discovery APIs and CLI outputs.

## Details
- Host discovery/introspect should include declared limits and current status (when available).
- `fcp connector info/introspect` should print limits in human and JSON outputs.
- Preflight should consider limits for would_succeed/availability.

## Tests
- Host discovery integration tests.
- CLI snapshot tests for list/info/introspect with limits.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can see limits before invoking operations.
- CLI output is stable and JSON schema includes limit metadata.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add canonicalization proof tests: permute fields -> canonical bytes identical.
- Add deterministic vector generator with fixed seeds and algebraic edge cases.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Batch ed25519 verification and reuse hashers where safe.
- Avoid allocations in canonicalization using stack buffers.

Evidence: `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/hazystream-host-ratelimit cargo test -p fcp-host --test rate_limit_integration -- --nocapture` passed (8 tests) and `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/hazystream-cli-ratelimit cargo test -p fcp-cli --test connector_introspect_test connector_introspect_json_includes_rate_limits -- --exact --nocapture` passed. This covers discovery/introspection rate-limit metadata on the host side and stable CLI JSON surfacing.","created_at":"2026-03-09T05:31:45Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ym1jq","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: canonicalize tag-rejection across all Value positions fuzz","description":"fcp_cbor::canonicalize_value_in_place (lib.rs:484-513) explicitly rejects every Value::Tag with UnsupportedTag — tags are not part of canonical CBOR per RFC 8949 §4.2 and could be used to smuggle non-canonical wire forms past the canonicalize gate. Existing canonicalize_map_deterministic explicitly bypasses tags ('we also avoid Tag since the canonicalizer rejects tags... not a canonicalization property to probe here'). NOT directly fuzzed across positions. Properties: Tag at root → UnsupportedTag with correct tag value; Tag inside Array element → UnsupportedTag (recursion catches); Tag as Map VALUE → UnsupportedTag; Tag as Map KEY → UnsupportedTag; Tag inside Tag (double-nested) → outer rejected first. Anchors: tag values 0, 1, 12345, u64::MAX at root + each container position. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:10:00.067502Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T23:11:34.337082Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T23:11:34.336581Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3132,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-ym1jq","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit 2e55984f. fuzz/fuzz_targets/cbor_tag_rejection.rs — 6 properties covering Tag rejection at all 5 structural positions (root, Array, Map value, Map key, Tag-in-Tag) + tag-free acceptance anchor. Once-gated anchors verify rejection at all positions for tag values {0, 1, 12345, u64::MAX} + structurally-twin tag-free acceptance. Existing canonicalize_map_deterministic explicitly skips tags; this fills that gap. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T23:11:33Z"}]}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yr0","title":"fcp.figma: Comment thread management","description":"# fcp.figma: Comment thread management (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable agents to manage Figma comment threads for design review workflows:
- list comments
- create comment
- reply
- resolve/unresolve

## Scope
### Read
- List comments for a file (pagination).

### Write (sensitive)
- Create comment on a node.
- Reply to a thread.
- Resolve/unresolve threads.
- Delete comment (dangerous; optional).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `figma.comments.read`
- `figma.comments.write` (dangerous-ish)
- Optional: `figma.comments.delete` (dangerous)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Comments contain human text and may contain sensitive info:
  - treat as tainted
  - never log raw comment bodies
- For writes:
  - receipts + audit events include file key + comment id

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `figma.comment.list`
  - `figma.comment.create`
  - `figma.comment.reply`
  - `figma.comment.resolve`
  - Optional: `figma.comment.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - pagination
  - write flows + audit/receipt
  - permission denied
  - log redaction

## Acceptance criteria
- Comment operations are safe, auditable, and do not leak content into logs.

Operations: figma.list_comments (with as_md option), figma.post_comment (with reply threading via comment_id), figma.delete_comment. 83 tests pass.","created_at":"2026-03-03T00:01:09Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yu0l5","title":"[PERF] fcp-raptorq::add_symbol: retries full decode on every post-K symbol","description":"crates/fcp-raptorq/src/decode.rs:119-145 and 151-234. Once received_count() >= K, add_symbol() calls try_reconstruct() on every newly arrived symbol. try_reconstruct() rebuilds decoder state, clones every buffered symbol into ReceivedSymbol rows, and allocates zero-filled virtual padding rows for K..K' on each attempt. If a decode needs K+R symbols, the total work becomes roughly sum_{i=K}^{K+R} O(i * symbol_size) instead of one decode plus bounded retries, which is a throughput cliff on lossy links. Proposed improvement: remember the last attempted symbol count / rank progress and only retry on meaningful new information (for example repair rows or a backoff schedule), or keep an incremental decoder state instead of rebuilding the full equation set on every symbol after K.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:48:59.607724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:59.404402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:59:59.404086Z","close_reason":"Added post-K retry coalescer to RaptorQDecoder::add_symbol (br-yu0l5). New state: last_attempted_at_count + post_k_retry_step (starts at 1, doubles on InsufficientSymbols, cap MAX_POST_K_RETRY_STEP=32). Decode attempts now fire only at (a) first K-crossover, (b) every repair-symbol arrival (ESI >= K; fresh LT equation), and (c) when received_count >= last_attempted + post_k_retry_step. Non-repair symbols between backoff ticks no longer trigger full try_reconstruct() rebuilds, eliminating the sum_{i=K}^{K+R} O(i * symbol_size) throughput cliff described in the bead. Regression tests: 5 new covering first crossover, repair-always-fires, source-only skip, threshold-reached trigger, backoff saturation arithmetic, plus round_trip_decode_after_backoff_coalesce_preserves_correctness (encode + decode a 640-byte payload via encoder.encode_all() and assert the full payload reconstructs cleanly — correctness regression harness). Verified: 756 fcp-raptorq lib tests pass (was 750 + 6 new). Pre-existing 4 failures (decoder_*_duplicate / slot_cap / cancellation) are unrelated and reproduce on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-yvul","title":"TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite","description":"# TEST: fcp-graphql Unit Test Suite


## Goal
Validate `fcp-graphql` via comprehensive deterministic unit tests against mocked schemas/endpoints, with structured logs.

## Scope
Comprehensive unit tests for GraphQL client infrastructure.

## Test Categories

### Query Execution
- [ ] Simple query execution
- [ ] Query with variables
- [ ] Mutations
- [ ] Subscriptions setup

### Query Building
- [ ] Fragment composition
- [ ] Nested selections
- [ ] Aliases
- [ ] Directives (@include, @skip)

### Pagination
- [ ] Cursor-based (Relay) pagination
- [ ] Offset-based pagination
- [ ] Automatic page traversal
- [ ] Total count handling

### Error Handling
- [ ] GraphQL errors parsing
- [ ] Partial data handling
- [ ] Network errors
- [ ] Timeout handling

### Batching & Deduplication
- [ ] Request batching
- [ ] Query deduplication
- [ ] Cache integration

### Schema Introspection
- [ ] Schema fetching
- [ ] Type validation
- [ ] Deprecation handling

## Mocking Strategy
- Mock GraphQL endpoints with wiremock
- Test against Linear-like, GitHub-like schemas
- Inject various error conditions

## Coverage Target: >90%

## Acceptance Criteria
- Runs deterministically in CI (no flake due to timing/network).
- Uses mocks only (no real external network).
- Emits structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35` with stable `test_name` identifiers.
